[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: returnFromPool(address _poolAddress, address _receiver, uint256 _amount) via StabilityPool.withdrawBNBGainToTrove] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER as a StabilityPool depositor under PRECONDITIONS where withdrawBNBGainToTrove updates snapshots and sends BNB to trove but does NOT call returnFromPool (USDS stays in SP) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: attacker calls withdrawBNBGainToTrove -> BNB gain transferred to trove -> deposit snapshots updated -> attacker then calls withdrawFromSP -> getCompoundedUSDSDeposit computed from new snapshots -> returnFromPool transfers USDS to attacker -> verify attacker did not receive BNB gain AND full USDS withdrawal in excess of entitlement, violating INVARIANT that total value extracted (BNB + USDS) must not exceed deposit value plus BNB gain entitlement, causing scoped impact: double-claiming BNB gain and full USDS withdrawal? Local proof idea: unit test calling withdrawBNBGainToTrove then withdrawFromSP; assert total value received <= initial deposit + BNB gain.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: mint(address _account, uint256 _amount) via BorrowerOperations._triggerBorrowingFee] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER as a borrower under PRECONDITIONS where getBorrowingFee returns a fee based on baseRate and oracleRate, and decayBaseRateFromBorrowing is called before getBorrowingFee trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: attacker calls openTrove with maxFeePercentage just above the decayed baseRate -> decayBaseRateFromBorrowing reduces baseRate -> getBorrowingFee computes lower fee -> _requireUserAcceptsFee passes with lower fee -> usdsToken.mint(sableStakingAddress, lowerFee) -> attacker pays less fee than expected at time of submission, violating INVARIANT that borrowing fee must be >= BORROWING_FEE_FLOOR * USDSAmount, causing scoped impact: attacker borrows USDS with below-floor fee, extracting value from stakers? Local proof idea: unit test timing openTrove to exploit baseRate decay window; assert USDSFee >= BORROWING_FEE_FLOOR * USDSAmount.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: burn(address _account, uint256 _amount) via BorrowerOperations._repayUSDS] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER as a borrower under PRECONDITIONS where _repayUSDS calls activePool.decreaseUSDSDebt(_USDS) then usdsToken.burn(_account, _USDS) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: attacker calls repayUSDS -> activePool.decreaseUSDSDebt executes -> if usdsToken.burn reverts (e.g., attacker's balance was drained between check and burn via a front-run transferFrom) -> activePool.USDSDebt is permanently decreased without corresponding USDS burn -> attacker can now borrow more USDS against the same collateral, violating INVARIANT that activePool.USDSDebt decrease must be atomic with usdsToken.burn, causing scoped impact: attacker creates phantom debt capacity enabling over-borrowing? Local proof idea: unit test with front-run that drains borrower balance between _requireSufficientUSDSBalance check and burn; assert activePool.USDSDebt decreased but totalSupply unchanged.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: transfer(address recipient, uint256 amount) / _requireValidRecipient] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS where _requireValidRecipient does not check if recipient is the sableStakingAddress trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: attacker calls usdsToken.transfer(sableStakingAddress, amount) -> USDS deposited into SableStakingV2 -> SableStakingV2.increaseF_USDS is NOT called so the accumulator is not updated -> stakers who claim rewards receive their F_USDS share from the accumulator but the extra USDS in the contract is unaccounted -> verify if SableStakingV2 distributes based on token balance or accumulator, violating INVARIANT that sableStaking USDS balance must equal sum of all pending F_USDS claims, causing scoped impact: stakers extract the donated USDS as bonus rewards? Local proof idea: unit test transferring USDS to sableStaking; call stake/unstake/claim; assert claimed amount > F_USDS accumulator share.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: mint(address _account, uint256 _amount) / burn(address _account, uint256 _amount)] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS where USDS_GAS_COMPENSATION is a system parameter that can be changed via TimeLock/SystemState trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: attacker opens trove when USDS_GAS_COMPENSATION = X -> USDS_GAS_COMPENSATION is changed to Y > X via governance -> attacker closes trove -> closeTrove burns debt.sub(USDS_GAS_COMPENSATION_new) from borrower and USDS_GAS_COMPENSATION_new from GasPool -> but GasPool only has X -> burn of Y from GasPool fails, violating INVARIANT that GasPool must always have sufficient USDS to cover gas compensation for all open troves, causing scoped impact: closeTrove permanently broken for troves opened before parameter change? Local proof idea: unit test openTrove with gasComp=X, change param to Y>X, call closeTrove; assert second burn reverts.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: permit(address owner, address spender, uint amount, uint deadline, uint8 v, bytes32 r, bytes32 s)] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS where permit uses _nonces[owner]++ (post-increment, nonce used then incremented) and the nonce is stored in a mapping trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: attacker front-runs a victim's permit transaction by submitting the same permit parameters first -> victim's permit succeeds (nonce was 0, now 1) -> attacker now has the allowance -> attacker calls transferFrom(victim, attacker, amount) before victim can revoke -> victim's intended use of the permit (e.g., repayUSDS) fails because allowance was consumed, violating INVARIANT that a permit signature can only be used by the intended recipient for the intended purpose, causing scoped impact: attacker steals victim's USDS by front-running permit? Local proof idea: unit test front-running permit; assert attacker can transferFrom victim after consuming permit nonce.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: mint(address _account, uint256 _amount) via BorrowerOperations.openTrove] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER as a borrower under PRECONDITIONS where openTrove in Recovery Mode skips _triggerBorrowingFee (no fee minted to sableStaking) but still mints _USDSAmount + USDS_GAS_COMPENSATION trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: attacker opens trove in Recovery Mode -> no fee minted -> attacker receives _USDSAmount USDS with zero borrowing fee -> attacker immediately closes trove (if allowed) or redeems -> attacker extracted USDS value without paying the borrowing fee that would normally compensate stakers, violating INVARIANT that all USDS minting must include appropriate fee payment to stakers except in explicitly fee-free scenarios, causing scoped impact: attacker borrows USDS for free in Recovery Mode, extracting value from stakers? Local proof idea: unit test openTrove in Recovery Mode; assert USDSFee == 0 and sableStaking received no fee; assert attacker net gain.
